<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The flashcards partial is a sibling element.

The clone logic appears correct. Flashcards may instead be nested inside notes-content or incorrectly included in the card pool.

The skip selector might fail on nested flashcards because `matches()` only checks direct children. The three possible interpretations are: hide flashcards in duo and trio displays, exclude the flashcards utility page from the pool, or avoid notes flagged for review.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `card.html` shortcode can embed flashcards directly in content. This likely explains why flashcards appear in random trio and duo displays.

The aside skip logic might fail if notes use `{{< card ...>}}` shortcodes instead of frontmatter `cards:`. Another possibility is that `cloneArticleBody` is not working correctly. Alternatively, notes with `review:true` may need to be excluded from the pool entirely.

The user likely means flashcards should not render when viewing a note in the duo or trio columns. The JavaScript already attempts to suppress these. A stale build may have prevented the recent `skipArticleSelector` update from taking effect. Alternatively, cloned content might still include flashcards from shortcodes.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: node -e &#34;
const fs=require(&#39;fs&#39;);
const html=fs.readFileSync(&#39;/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/capture/index.html&#39;,&#39;utf8&#39;);
const {JSDOM}=require(&#39;jsdom&#39;);
const doc=new JSDOM(html).window.document;
const article=doc.querySelector(&#39;article&#39;);
const skip=&#39;.notes-graph-panel, .notes-toolbar, .article-title, .toc-wrapper, .notes-outgoing, .notes-see-also, .notes-backlinks, .notes-note-flashcards&#39;;
let cloned=[];
for (const child of article.children) {
  if (child.matches(skip)) { cloned.push(&#39;SKIP:&#39;+child.className.split(&#39; &#39;)[0]); continue; }
  cloned.push(&#39;CLONE:&#39;+(child.className||child.tagName));
}
console.log(cloned.join(&#39;\n&#39;));
console.log(&#39;flashcards in clone:&#39;, doc.querySelector(&#39;.notes-note-flashcards&#39;) ? &#39;in source&#39; : &#39;no&#39;);
&#34;</summary>
```bash
node -e "
const fs=require('fs');
const html=fs.readFileSync('/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/capture/index.html','utf8');
const {JSDOM}=require('jsdom');
const doc=new JSDOM(html).window.document;
const article=doc.querySelector('article');
const skip='.notes-graph-panel, .notes-toolbar, .article-title, .toc-wrapper, .notes-outgoing, .notes-see-also, .notes-backlinks, .notes-note-flashcards';
let cloned=[];
for (const child of article.children) {
  if (child.matches(skip)) { cloned.push('SKIP:'+child.className.split(' ')[0]); continue; }
  cloned.push('CLONE:'+(child.className||child.tagName));
}
console.log(cloned.join('\n'));
console.log('flashcards in clone:', doc.querySelector('.notes-note-flashcards') ? 'in source' : 'no');
"
```

from html.parser import HTMLParser
import re

# find article section - crude
m = re.search(r&#39;&lt;article&gt;(.*?)&lt;/article&gt;&#39;, html, re.DOTALL)
if not m:
    print(&#39;no article&#39;)
else:
    art = m.group(1)
    # find direct child opening tags at shallow level
    for tag in re.finditer(r&#39;&lt;(aside|div|h1|nav|section)[^&gt;]*class=&#34;([^&#34;]*)&#34;&#39;, art[:5000]):
        print(tag.group(1), tag.group(2)[:80])

    print(&#39;has notes-note-flashcards:&#39;, &#39;notes-note-flashcards&#39; in art)
PY</summary>
```bash
python3 << 'PY'
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import re
html = open('/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/capture/index.html').read()
# find article section - crude
m = re.search(r'<article>(.*?)</article>', html, re.DOTALL)
if not m:
    print('no article')
else:
    art = m.group(1)
    # find direct child opening tags at shallow level
    for tag in re.finditer(r'<(aside|div|h1|nav|section)[^>]*class="([^"]*)"', art[:5000]):
        print(tag.group(1), tag.group(2)[:80])
    print('---')
    print('has notes-note-flashcards:', 'notes-note-flashcards' in art)
PY
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 -c &#34;
from html.parser import HTMLParser
html=open(&#39;/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/capture/index.html&#39;).read()
# use regex to find article and list top-level elements
import re
start=html.find(&#39;&lt;article&gt;&#39;)
end=html.find(&#39;&lt;/article&gt;&#39;, start)
art=html[start:end]
# strip nested - count depth
depth=0
i=0
children=[]
while i &lt; len(art):
    if art[i:i+2]==&#39;&lt;/&#39;:
        depth-=1
        i=art.find(&#39;&gt;&#39;, i)+1
        continue
    if art[i]==&#39;&lt;&#39;:
        j=art.find(&#39;&gt;&#39;, i)
        tag=art[i+1:j]
        if not tag.startswith(&#39;!&#39;):
            if depth==0 and not tag.startswith(&#39;/&#39;):
                name=tag.split()[0]
                cls=re.search(r&#39;class=\&#34;([^\&#34;]*)\&#34;&#39;, tag)
                children.append((name, cls.group(1) if cls else &#39;&#39;))
            if not tag.endswith(&#39;/&#39;) and not tag.startswith(&#39;!&#39;):
                depth+=1
        i=j+1
    else:
        i+=1
for c in children:
    print(c[0], c[1][:60])
&#34;
</summary>
```bash
python3 -c "
from html.parser import HTMLParser
html=open('/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/capture/index.html').read()
# use regex to find article and list top-level elements
import re
start=html.find('<article>')
end=html.find('</article>', start)
art=html[start:end]
# strip nested - count depth
depth=0
i=0
children=[]
while i < len(art):
    if art[i:i+2]=='</':
        depth-=1
        i=art.find('>', i)+1
        continue
    if art[i]=='<':
        j=art.find('>', i)
        tag=art[i+1:j]
        if not tag.startswith('!'):
            if depth==0 and not tag.startswith('/'):
                name=tag.split()[0]
                cls=re.search(r'class=\"([^\"]*)\"', tag)
                children.append((name, cls.group(1) if cls else ''))
            if not tag.endswith('/') and not tag.startswith('!'):
                depth+=1
        i=j+1
    else:
        i+=1
for c in children:
    print(c[0], c[1][:60])
"

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
cloneArticleBody copies notes metadata, tags, issues, content, outgoing links, see-also, and backlinks, but deliberately skips flashcards.

A cleaner fix is to clone only `.notes-content`. Flashcards sit outside that container, so this avoids the fragile exclude list entirely.

The notes-graph-panel should also stay hidden. Outgoing links, see-also, and backlinks likely belong in the same exclusion. Toolbar handling needs verification.

The skip list already excludes outgoing links, backlinks, and flashcards. The remaining concern is whether `matches()` fails to catch flashcards. The simplest fix is to replace child iteration with a direct clone of the content element itself.

A concrete `cloneNoteBody` helper is drafted: it selects `.notes-content`, clones it directly, and returns a new wrapper div. This removes the need for a skip list entirely. The next step is to check git history to understand why the original function was designed around child iteration.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking git history to see if the notes-note-flashcards skip list entry is missing or uncommitted.

The skip list entry is already committed. The note-flashcards partial was likely added recently without updating the skip selector. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Random Duo and Trio now clone only the note body (`.notes-content`) instead of filtering direct children of `<article>`.

**What changed**

Previously, the skip-list approach cloned most article children and tried to exclude the flashcard block via `.notes-note-flashcards`. That was fragile — flashcards sit *outside* `.notes-content`, but the old approach still cloned graph panels, meta, tags, and issue panels into each column.

Now both `notes-random-duo.js` and `notes-random-trio.js` use `cloneNoteContent()`, which:

1. Clones only `.notes-content` (prose body)
2. Strips any `.notes-flashcard` / `.notes-note-flashcards` nodes inside that clone (covers inline shortcode cards)

Flashcard decks no longer appear in the side-by-side columns. The AI prompt was already using `.notes-content` text only, so it stays flashcard-free.
